slides.title: Walk backward to train *body and brain*.
slides.sub: Reverse walking loads your knees in a fresh pattern and sharpens balance. Here is why it works.

slides.title: An old pattern, reversed
slides.body: Most of us only ever move forward. Walking in reverse asks your joints and mind to *work in a new way*.

slides.title: Knees, loaded differently
slides.body: Reverse steps shift force across the knee in a fresh pattern. This *may help* the joint move and feel better.

slides.title: Balance gets a workout
slides.body: Without your usual cues, the body leans on smaller stabilizers. That challenge *can support* steadier balance over time.

slides.title: Your brain pays attention
slides.body: Moving blind to what is behind you demands focus. The extra attention *trains coordination* alongside the muscles.

slides.title: Small and steady
slides.body: Begin on a flat, clear path or treadmill at low speed. A few short minutes *is enough* to start.

slides.title: Make every step *count*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your steps, movement, sleep and food, turning the habits that keep you mobile into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Walking forward is easy. Walking backward is where the joints and the brain both get to grow. 🌿

Reverse walking loads the knees in a new pattern, taxes your balance and asks the brain to pay attention, all at once. Start small and steady. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your daily movement into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Walking Backward Trains Your Joints and Brain
